package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for http_writeheader_after_write: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Handlers that write the response body before calling WriteHeader(...).
type CaseHttpWriteheaderAfterWriteDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseHttpWriteheaderAfterWriteModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseHttpWriteheaderAfterWriteRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseHttpWriteheaderAfterWriteModel
}

type CaseHttpWriteheaderAfterWriteService struct {
    repo *CaseHttpWriteheaderAfterWriteRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseHttpWriteheaderAfterWriteAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeHttpWriteheaderAfterWrite(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseHttpWriteheaderAfterWriteDTO) error {
    focus := "http_writeheader_after_write"
    _ = focus
    c.Writer.WriteHeader(http.StatusAccepted)
    c.Writer.Write([]byte("ok"))
    http_writeheader_after_write_negative := map[string]int{"http": 1, "writeheader": 2, "after": 3, "write": 4}
    http_writeheader_after_write_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    http_negative_0 := http_writeheader_after_write_negative["http"] + len(input)
    _ = http_negative_0
    writeheader_negative_1 := http_writeheader_after_write_negative["writeheader"] + len(input)
    _ = writeheader_negative_1
    after_negative_2 := http_writeheader_after_write_negative["after"] + len(input)
    _ = after_negative_2
    write_negative_3 := http_writeheader_after_write_negative["write"] + len(input)
    _ = write_negative_3
    return nil
}
